index.name: Nifty CPSE Index
index.description: Nifty CPSE (Central Public Sector Enterprise) Index tracks 12 largest government-owned companies, launched Dec 2014 as part of Government Disinvestment Program — pioneering use of ETF for stake sales (₹40K Cr raised through tranches). Current holdings: ONGC (20.3%), Coal India (18.9%), NTPC (16.4%), Power Grid (12.3%), BEL (10.2%), GAIL (8.4%), IOC (6.8%), BHEL (3.8%). Ultra-low expense ratio 0.01% makes it India's cheapest equity ETF. Three investment theses: (1) Dividend yield play: PSU index yields 5-6% annually via mandatory dividend payouts from government; (2) Valuation recovery: PSUs trade at 0.8-1.0x book (vs Nifty 50 valuations 2.5-3.0x book), suggesting 3-4x upside if P/BV normalizes; (3) Capex leverage: ₹9 Lakh Cr government capex (FY25-27) flows through PSU order books, driving earnings 15-20% CAGR. Government shareholding: 50-60% ensures dividend commitment (no free cash flow diversion). Reform tailwinds: privatization talk of BHEL/Air India creates optionality; Disinvestment Commissioner replacing PSU heads with professional management improving ROE. Risk: political interference in dividend policy (if government needs fiscal space, dividend cuts enacted), commodity price collapses (Coal India, ONGC sensitive to global prices), and interest rate hikes (PSU capex financing costs rise). Offered typically at 5-10% discount during ETF NFO tranches.

holdings.name: ONGC
holdings.description: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation — India's largest oil producer. Produces 70% of India's domestic crude oil and 62% of natural gas. Government holds 58% stake. Consistent dividend payer with high dividend yield. Owns HPCL (downstream) and ONGC Videsh for overseas exploration.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: World's largest coal mining company. Produces 700+ million tonnes annually — monopoly in Indian coal sector. Government holds 63% stake. Dividend yield of 6-8%. Essential for India's thermal power generation and energy security.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: India's largest power generation utility with 73 GW operational capacity. Government holds 51% stake. Transitioning to renewables: 60 GW renewable target by 2032. Regulated returns, consistent dividends and government backing provide stability.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corp
holdings.description: India's monopoly inter-state power transmission utility. Government holds 51%. Owns 70% of India's inter-state transmission network (175,000+ circuit km). Regulated 15.5% post-tax returns. Building green energy corridors.

holdings.name: Bharat Electronics
holdings.description: Defence electronics PSU making radar, sonar, communication and electronic warfare systems. Government holds 51.14%. ₹65,000+ Cr order backlog with 5+ year visibility. Growing export business to SE Asia and Middle East.

holdings.name: GAIL India
holdings.description: India's largest natural gas marketing and transmission company. 12,000+ km cross-country pipeline network. Government holds 51%. Beneficiary of India's city gas distribution (CGD) expansion and natural gas economy push.

holdings.name: Indian Oil Corporation
holdings.description: India's largest oil company by revenue. 11 refineries (80 MMTPA capacity), 33,000+ fuel stations, Indane LPG and SERVO lubricants brands. Government holds 52%. India's highest revenue company and Fortune 500 entrant.

holdings.name: BHEL
holdings.description: Bharat Heavy Electricals — India's largest power equipment manufacturer. Makes turbines, generators, transformers and boilers. Government holds 63%. Reviving order book from thermal power and defence electronics diversification.

holdings.name: Container Corp of India
holdings.description: India's largest container rail logistics company operating 89 rail terminals across India. Government holds 54% via Ministry of Railways. Beneficiary of dedicated freight corridors (DFC) improving container rail efficiency.

name: Mirae Asset Mutual Fund - Mirae Asset Nifty India Manufacturing ETF

index.name: Nifty India Manufacturing Total Return Index
index.description: Mirae Asset Mutual Fund - Mirae Asset Nifty India Manufacturing ETF tracks the Nifty India Manufacturing Total Return Index index.

holdings.name: Larsen & Toubro
holdings.description: India's largest engineering conglomerate. Defence, nuclear, railways, semiconductor fab projects. Nifty India Manufacturing anchor.

holdings.name: HAL
holdings.description: India's sole military aircraft manufacturer. LCA Tejas and Dhruv helicopter production. Government holds 71%. Rs 1.3 lakh Cr order book.

holdings.name: Siemens India
holdings.description: Digital industries, smart infrastructure and mobility. Power T&D and railway systems. Germany-parent technology transfer.

holdings.name: ABB India
holdings.description: Power grids, industrial automation and robotics. Electrification products — transformers, motors, drives for infrastructure build-out.

holdings.name: BEL
holdings.description: Radar, sonar, electronic warfare manufacturing PSU. Rs 65,000 Cr backlog. Exports to 25+ countries. Government holds 51%.

holdings.name: Bharat Forge
holdings.description: Global forged components leader for trucks, passenger cars and defence vehicles. European and North American supply chain.

holdings.name: Kaynes Technology
holdings.description: Aerospace, defence and medical EMS. ITAR-certified for US defence contracts. Highest growth EMS play in Indian manufacturing.

holdings.name: Dixon Technologies
holdings.description: PLI electronics manufacturer — smartphones, LED TVs, washing machines. India's largest CE contract manufacturer.

holdings.name: Thermax
holdings.description: Energy and environment solutions — boilers, heat exchangers, green hydrogen equipment. Industrial decarbonisation and export growth.

holdings.name: CG Power
holdings.description: Transformers, switchgear, motors and railway traction. Murugappa Group turnaround. Railway electrification driving order wins.
